description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:150-165 (der_length) accepts long-form length encodings even when short form would suffice (e.g. 0x81 0x05 instead of 0x05). Strict DER (X.690 §10.1) requires the shortest encoding. The function also accepts long-form lengths up to 2 bytes but does not reject 0x81 with a value < 0x80 (which would be a non-minimal encoding).

Trigger: a DER signature crafted with non-minimal length encoding parses successfully and produces a valid raw signature.

Bad behavior: this is not exploitable via the AWS KMS service path (KMS produces strict DER), but ecdsa_der_to_raw_p256 is reachable from any caller who can supply a byte slice. Future contributors may copy this DER parser into a context where the input is attacker-controlled, propagating the laxity.

Severity is bounded because:
  - Output is always a 64-byte raw signature; any signature that round-trips through DER is verifiable independently against the public key, so a 'malformed' DER cannot cause a verification false positive.
  - There is no observable signature-malleability in the raw output: r and s are already canonical 32-byte big-endian.

This is more 'protect future copy/paste' than 'fix today's bug'. P1 not P0 because no current caller path supplies untrusted DER.
title: ECDSA DER parser does not enforce minimal-length encoding
